Title: User Login
Preconditions: User account exists in the system.
Test Data: Valid user credentials (email: user@example.com, password: password123).
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login screen.
2. Enter valid email and password.
3. Click on the login button.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the home page.

Title: User Logout
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the user profile page.
2. Click on the logout button.
Expected Result: User is logged out and redirected to the login screen.

Title: Add User to Faculty
Preconditions: Faculty account exists in the system.
Test Data: Valid user details (name: John Doe, email: john.doe@example.com).
Test Steps:
1. Log in as a faculty member.
2. Navigate to the user management page.
3. Click on the addUser() operation.
4. Enter valid user details.
5. Submit the form.
Expected Result: User is added to the faculty and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Remove User from Faculty
Preconditions: User is assigned to a faculty.
Test Steps:
1. Log in as a faculty member.
2. Navigate to the user management page.
3. Select the user to be removed.
4. Click on the removeUser() operation.
5. Confirm removal.
Expected Result: User is removed from the faculty and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Assign User to Camp
Preconditions: User and camp exist in the system.
Test Data: Valid user details (name: Jane Smith, email: jane.smith@example.com), valid camp details (name: Summer Camp).
Test Steps:
1. Log in as a faculty member.
2. Navigate to the user management page.
3. Click on the addUser() operation.
4. Enter valid user details and select the camp.
5. Submit the form.
Expected Result: User is assigned to the specified camp and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Remove User from Camp
Preconditions: User is assigned to a camp.
Test Steps:
1. Log in as a faculty member.
2. Navigate to the user management page.
3. Select the user to be removed.
4. Click on the removeUser() operation.
5. Confirm removal.
Expected Result: User is removed from the camp and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Access Menu and Navigate to Monitoring Section
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the home page.
Test Steps:
1. Click on the menu at the bottom of the home page.
2. Select the monitoring section.
Expected Result: User is navigated to the monitoring section.

Title: Return to Home Page from Monitoring Section
Preconditions: User is in the monitoring section.
Test Steps: 1. Click on the return to home option.
Expected Result: User is navigated back to the home page.

Title: Successful Login
Preconditions: User account exists in the system.
Test Data: Valid user credentials (email: user@example.com, password: password123).
Test Steps:
1. Open the login activity.
2. Enter valid email and password.
3. Click on the login button.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the home activity.

Title: Invalid Credentials

2. Call the replaceFragment() method with a valid fragment.
Expected Result: The specified fragment is replaced and displayed.

2. Verify profile information is displayed correctly.

Title: Access Notes
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the menu note fragment.
Test Steps:
1. Open the menu note fragment.
2. Verify notes are accessible.
Expected Result: User can access and view notes.

Title: Navigate Through Various Fragments
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the home activity.
Test Steps:
1. Open the home activity.
2. Navigate to the profile activity.
3. From the profile activity, navigate to the graph sono fragment.
4. From the graph sono fragment, navigate to the graph alattamento fragment.
5. From the graph alattamento fragment, navigate to the graph crescita fragment.
6. From the graph crescita fragment, navigate to the sono fragment.
7. From the sono fragment, navigate to the crescita fragment.
Expected Result: User can navigate through all specified fragments without errors.

Title: Return to Home After Viewing Notes
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the menu note fragment.
Test Steps:
1. Open the menu note fragment.
2. Click on the return to home option.
Expected Result: User is navigated back to the home page.
